Gender Equality.reason: The syllabus includes dedicated sessions on women writers, the "New Woman" (新女性), and "gay literature" (同志文學), directly addressing gender and sexuality issues (Targets 5.1, 5.c).
evidence: 電影欣賞 ：《新女性》與《現代一女性》

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The reading of Yang Kui's "The Newspaper Man" (〈送報伕〉), a work of proletarian literature, introduces themes of labor and exploitation, touching on labor rights (Target 8.8).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: The course fosters "social concern" and analyzes inequalities through topics like "left-wing worldism," "history and trauma," and "gay literature," promoting social inclusion (Target 10.2).

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: The course analyzes literary representations of urban life in sections like "Modernism and the City" (現代派與都市), contributing to safeguarding cultural heritage (Target 11.4).
evidence: 現代派與都市(一)：上海新感覺派小說
現代派與都市(二)：上海心理小說

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course analyzes historical injustice and violence through literature, specifically "writing about the 228" incident, which relates to peace and justice (Targets 16.1, 16.3).
evidence: 歷史與創傷：書寫二二八

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course incorporates guest lectures from an external scholar and a writer, demonstrating a partnership to share knowledge and expertise (Target 17.16).
